messages.content:
What does it mean if a substance is soluble?
￼
A.
It is a liquid.
￼
B.
It produces a gas.
￼
C.
It dissolves in water.
￼
D.
It is a solid.
C. It dissolves in water.

Which of the following reactions demonstrates a synthesis reaction?
￼
A.
2NaCl ￼ 2Na + Cl2
￼
B.
Mg + H2SO4 ￼ MgSO4 + H2
￼
C.
Zn + 2HCl ￼ ZnCl2 + H2
￼
D.
C + O2 ￼ CO2
D. C + O2 ￼ CO2.

Which metal can replace another metal in a reaction?
￼
A.
A metal lower on the activity series list will replace one that is higher.
￼
B.
Any metal on the list can replace any other metal also on the list.
A. A metal lower on the activity series list will replace one that is higher.

Which metal can replace another metal in a reaction?
￼
A.
A metal lower on the activity series list will replace one that is higher.
￼
B.
Any metal on the list can replace any other metal also on the list.￼
C.
A metal higher on the activity series list will replace one that is lower.
￼
D.
A metal on the activity series list will replace one that is not on the list.
A. A metal lower on the activity series list will replace one that is higher.

What kind of reaction is 2H2O2 ￼ 2H2O + O2?
￼
A.
Synthesis
￼
B.
Decomposition
￼
C.
Single-replacement
￼
D.
Double-replacement
B. Decomposition.

What kind of reaction happens when two compounds switch elements?
￼
A.
A synthesis reaction
￼
B.
A double-replacement reaction
￼
C.
A single-replacement reaction
￼
D.
A decomposition reaction
B. A double-replacement reaction.

What is formed in the reaction KI(aq) + AgNO3(aq) ￼ AgI(s) + KNO3(aq)?
￼
A.
A gas
￼
B.
Water
￼
C.
A mixture
￼
D.
A precipitate
D. A precipitate.

What kind of reaction is represented by Fe + CuSO4 ￼ Cu + FeSO4?
￼
A.
A single-replacement reaction
￼
B.
A double-replacement reaction
￼
C.
A synthesis reaction
￼
D.
A decomposition reaction
A. A single-replacement reaction.

What kind of reaction is represented by Fe + CuSO4 ￼ Cu + FeSO4?
￼
A.
A single-replacement reaction
￼
B.
A double-replacement reaction
￼
C.
A synthesis reaction
￼
D.
A decomposition reaction
A. A single-replacement reaction.

What is the net ionic equation of Mg(s) + Zn2+ + 2NO3- ￼ Mg2+ + 2NO3- + Zn(s)?
￼
A.
Mg(NO3)2 + Zn(s) ￼ Zn2+ + 2NO3- + Mg(s)
￼
B.
Mg(s) + Zn2+ ￼ Mg2+ + Zn(s)
￼
C.
Mg(s) + Zn(NO3)2 (aq) ￼ Mg(NO3 )2(aq) + Zn(s)
￼
D.
Mg(s) ￼ Zn(s)
B. Mg(s) + Zn2+ ￼ Mg2+ + Zn(s)

What is an organic chemical?
￼
A.
A chemical made of a metal and a nonmetal
￼
B.
A diatomic molecule that exists in gas form
￼
C.
A chemical that makes up rocks and minerals
￼
D.
A chemical that forms parts of living things
D. A chemical that forms parts of living things.

What is an organic chemical?
￼
A.
A chemical made of a metal and a nonmetal
￼
B.
A diatomic molecule that exists in gas form
￼
C.
A chemical that makes up rocks and minerals
￼
D.
A chemical that forms parts of living things￼
C.
All ionic substances are written with the state symbol (aq) after it.
￼
D.
All substances are labeled with the oxidation states of the atoms.
D. A chemical that forms parts of living things.

Which of the following best describes a balanced reaction?
￼
A.
A reaction that has the same type of atoms on each side of the equation
￼
B.
A reaction that has the same number of atoms on each side of the equation￼
C.
A reaction that has the same number of molecules on each side of the equation
￼
D.
A reaction that has the same number and type of atoms on each side of the equation
D. A reaction that has the same number and type of atoms on each side of the equation.

What are the spectator ions in Mg(s) + Zn2+ + 2NO3- ￼ Mg2+ + 2NO3- + Zn(s)?
￼
A.
NO3-
￼
B.
NO3-, Zn2+, and Mg2+
￼
C.
Zn2+ and Mg2+
￼
D.
Mg(s) and Zn(s)
A. NO3-.

What are the spectator ions in Mg(s) + Zn2+ + 2NO3- ￼ Mg2+ + 2NO3- + Zn(s)?
￼
A.
NO3-
￼
B.
NO3-, Zn2+, and Mg2+
￼
C.
Zn2+ and Mg2+
￼
D.
Mg(s) and Zn(s)
A. NO3-.

What is the complete ionic equation for H2SO4(aq) + CaI2(aq) ￼ CaSO4(s) + 2HI(aq)?
￼
A.
2H+ + SO42- + Ca2+ + 2I- ￼ CaSO4 + 2H+ + 2I-
￼
B.
H++ SO42- + Ca2+ + 2I- ￼ CaSO4 + H+ + I-￼
C.
SO42- + Ca2+ ￼ CaSO4
￼
D.
2H+ + SO42- + Ca2+ + 2I- ￼ Ca2+ + SO42- + 2H+ + 2I-
